chief_complaint: A 70-year-old male presented with acute painless hematochezia.
history: The patient had a history of colonic diverticulosis and presented with sudden onset of hematochezia. He had no significant past medical history and was not on any medications.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed a tender abdomen with no guarding or rebound tenderness.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: Hb 8.5 g/dL, MCV 90 fL, platelets 200 x 10^9/L. Colonoscopy showed active bleeding originating from a diverticulum.
differentials: Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to angiodysplasia
Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to ischemic colitis
Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to colorectal cancer
Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to inflammatory bowel disease
correct_ranking: Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to angiodysplasia
Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to ischemic colitis
Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to colorectal cancer
Lower gastrointestinal bleeding due to inflammatory bowel disease
